summaryText: Pastor Todd Boffo opened the message 'Character is the Canvas of Legacy' by reflecting on the life of King David. He taught that a person's legacy is not about being impressive or perfect, but about living a life of purpose anchored in godly character. Using the metaphor of a tree, he explained that our gifts and talents are the visible fruit, but our character is the hidden root system that determines our strength and longevity. David's life exemplified this, as he was both a warrior and a worshipper, a man after God's own heart who experienced great triumphs and profound failures. Pastor Todd emphasized that God's grace is greater than our greatest mistakes and that true character is forged and strengthened through the process of life, repentance, and returning to God. He challenged the congregation to focus on building their inner life—being 'bigger on the inside than you are on the outside'—rather than just curating their external image. The sermon concluded with a call to allow God to shape our character in private seasons of preparation, to use our past battles as testimonies for future victories, and to intentionally build a legacy that is transferred to the next generation.
mainPoints: Legacy is built on purpose and faithfulness, not on perfection or external impressiveness.
Godly character is shaped in private, hidden seasons of preparation and obedience.
A lasting legacy is about who you are becoming in Christ and what you transfer to others, not just what you accomplish.

versesMentioned.text: Now when David had served God’s purpose in his own generation, he fell asleep; he was buried with his ancestors and his body decayed.
A good man brings good things out of the good stored up in his heart, and an evil man brings evil things out of the evil stored up in his heart. For the mouth speaks what the heart is full of.

David also said to Solomon his son, 'Be strong and courageous, and do the work. Do not be afraid or discouraged, for the LORD God, my God, is with you. He will not fail you or forsake you until all the work for the service of the temple of the LORD is finished.'

But the LORD said to Samuel, 'Do not consider his appearance or his height, for I have rejected him. The LORD does not look at the things people look at. People look at the outward appearance, but the LORD looks at the heart.'
So he asked Jesse, 'Are these all the sons you have?' 'There is still the youngest,' Jesse answered. 'He is tending the sheep.' Samuel said, 'Send for him; we will not sit down until he arrives.'

Monday.scripture: 1 Samuel 16:7 - 'People look at the outward appearance, but the LORD looks at the heart.'
Monday.action: Spend a few minutes in quiet reflection, asking God to show you one area of your inner life (your heart) that He wants to strengthen.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to value what You value. Shift my focus today from managing my external image to cultivating a heart that pleases You.

Tuesday.scripture: Acts 13:36 - 'David had served God’s purpose in his own generation...'
Tuesday.action: Write down one simple way you can serve God's purpose in your current sphere of influence (e.g., family, work, community) today.
Tuesday.prayer: God, thank You that my life has purpose. Help me to see and faithfully serve Your purpose for me in my generation.

Wednesday.scripture: Luke 6:45 - 'For the mouth speaks what the heart is full of.'
Wednesday.action: Pay attention to your words today. What do they reveal about what's in your heart? Commit to filling your heart with God's truth through a few minutes of Scripture reading.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, make me aware of what fills my heart. Purify my thoughts and let my words today reflect Your goodness and grace.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Chronicles 28:20 - 'Be strong and courageous... for the LORD God, my God, is with you.'
Thursday.action: Identify one person in the next generation (a child, mentee, younger friend) you can encourage with a word of affirmation or prayer today.
Thursday.prayer: Father, give me courage to build and invest in others. Help me to transfer faith and encouragement to the next generation, trusting that You are with me.

Friday.scripture: 1 Samuel 16:11 - 'There is still the youngest... He is tending the sheep.'
Friday.action: Faithfully complete a routine or unseen task today with a heart of worship, offering it to God as part of your preparation.
Friday.prayer: God, when I feel overlooked or in a hidden season, remind me that You see me. Help me to be faithful in small things, trusting Your timing and anointing.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 51:10 (Implied from David's repentance) - 'Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.'
Saturday.action: Look back on your week. In a journal or quiet moment, ask God: 'What is one 'baby step' I can take next week to strengthen the character canvas of my life?'
Saturday.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your forgiveness and grace. You are the master artist of my life. Take the canvas of my character and shape it according to Your will, for Your glory.
